name: Fried Eggplant Sandwiches
recipeInstructions:
1. Slice the eggplant into 1/4-inch rounds.
2. Sprinkle with salt and let drain in a colander for about an hour.
3. Slice the mozzarella into thin slices and trim to the shape of the eggplant slices.
4. Dissolve yeast over warm water (105-115 degrees) and stir until dissolved.
5. Whisk together 1 1/2 cups flour, pepper, and 1 cup warm water until smooth.
6. Keep mixture at room temperature for 30 minutes.
7. Rinse and pat dry the eggplant slices.
8. Fry eggplant slices in batches using oil (about 1 cup per batch) over medium heat about 2 minutes per side.
9. Remove to paper towels to drain while finishing other slices.
10. Place a cheese slice between each two eggplant slices (like a sandwich).
11. Press firmly together and dip in the remaining flour to coat both sides.
12. Beat the egg white until stiff, but not dry peaks form.
13. Fold into the yeast mixture.
14. Heat oil (about 1 inch deep) in a large frying pan.
15. Dip 1 eggplant sandwich into the batter at a time and place in the hot oil.
16. Fry 4-6 sandwiches at a time for 1-2 minutes per side until light brown.
17. Place on paper towels to drain excess oil before serving.
18. Serve hot. These are great with a horseradish type dressing on top! Depending on the size of the eggplant, this could make 6-12 sandwiches.
